verblock: "24 Apr 2026:v0.2: matts - Phase 0 canon decisions"
intent_version: 2.9.0
---

# ST0035 Design — Canonical LLM Config

This document captures the canon decisions for Intent's LLM-facing configuration surface in v2.9.1+. It is the "why" companion to `info.md`'s "what" and the per-WP `info.md`'s "how". Decisions are numbered `D1`..`Dn` for cross-reference.

## D1. AGENTS.md is the primary LLM config file

**Decision.** Every Intent project ships a root-level `AGENTS.md`. It is auto-generated by `intent agents sync`, written as a real file (not a symlink), and is the first file every LLM-facing tool reads.

**Why.** The community converged on AGENTS.md in 2025-2026. 60k+ repos, Linux Foundation Agentic AI Foundation governance, uniform adoption across Anthropic / OpenAI / Google / Cursor / GitHub Copilot / Codex / Aider / Continue / Cline / Gemini CLI / VS Code. Deviating costs Intent users compatibility with tooling they already rely on. Aligning is free.

**Why real file, not symlink.** Symlinks work for tools that follow them, but some tooling treats them opaquely (CI systems, web-rendered docs, `cat` via `find`). A real file is simpler, matches community norm, and removes a class of bug. The small cost is that `intent agents sync` must write to two locations if the `intent/llm/AGENTS.md` layer ever comes back; it won't.

**Consequences.**

- `intent/llm/AGENTS.md` retires (WP10).
- `intent/plugins/agents/bin/intent_agents` writes to root (WP08).
- Projects with a root `AGENTS.md` symlink get it replaced by a real file during `intent claude upgrade --apply`.
- Arca/\*'s symlink convention stops; they get real files (WP16 rollout).

## D2. CLAUDE.md is a Claude-specific overlay, not a competitor

**Decision.** Every Intent project optionally has a root-level `CLAUDE.md`. It imports `AGENTS.md` by reference (does not duplicate content) and adds Claude-specific directives: memory directory, file mappings, `/in-session` auto-load wiring, pointer to `.claude/settings.json` hooks. Templated; user-editable after init.

**Why.** Anthropic's official position: CLAUDE.md complements AGENTS.md. If both exist, CLAUDE.md rules win _for Claude sessions only_. Treating them as parallel sources of truth creates divergence; treating CLAUDE.md as an overlay removes that risk. Claude-specific features (memories, `/in-*` slash commands, `.claude/settings.json` hooks) belong here; tool-agnostic project knowledge belongs in AGENTS.md.

**Consequences.**

- `lib/templates/llm/_CLAUDE.md` becomes an overlay template, not a standalone guide (WP09).
- Content duplicated from AGENTS.md is removed; cross-references added.
- Intent's own `CLAUDE.md` is rewritten accordingly (WP13).

## D3. usage-rules.md stays and is honoured

**Decision.** Root `usage-rules.md` is kept. Refreshed to current-as-built state (WP02) but not deleted. Intent-authored template lands at `lib/templates/llm/_usage-rules.md` and is planted in downstream projects by `intent claude upgrade --apply`.

**Why.** The Elixir ecosystem has a mature per-package `usage-rules.md` convention: each package ships a `usage-rules.md`, and `mix usage_rules.sync` gathers them into a project-level AGENTS.md. No equivalent yet exists for Python / Node / Ruby / Rust. Intent runs Elixir fleet projects (Lamplight, Laksa, Anvil, the Arca trio, Conflab, MeetZaya, MicroGPTEx, Multiplyer, Prolix) that already benefit from this pattern. Removing `usage-rules.md` would break alignment with Elixir's most mature LLM-guidance convention for no gain.

**Why a separate file from AGENTS.md.** AGENTS.md is broad and auto-generated; `usage-rules.md` is terse, prescriptive, and hand-maintained. Rough split:

- `usage-rules.md` = rules I must follow (DO / NEVER contract).
- `AGENTS.md` = how to navigate this project (overview, commands, skills, subagents).

They co-exist cleanly. Downstream Elixir tooling discovers `usage-rules.md` the same way it discovers package rules.

**Consequences.**

- Root `usage-rules.md` is refreshed with v2.9.0+ surface (/in-\* skills, extensions, critics, hooks).
- A new template at `lib/templates/llm/_usage-rules.md` becomes the starting point for rollout.
- `mix usage_rules.sync` continues to work in Elixir projects without any Intent interference.

## D4. intent/llm/ keeps MODULES.md and DECISION_TREE.md only

**Decision.** `intent/llm/` becomes strictly the internal-enforcement layer: `MODULES.md` (Highlander registry), `DECISION_TREE.md` (code-placement flowchart). No `AGENTS.md` here anymore. No `_llm_preamble.md` (retired everywhere).

**Why.** `intent/llm/MODULES.md` and `intent/llm/DECISION_TREE.md` serve a different audience (Intent's own developers) than the root files (downstream users and LLM tooling). Mixing them in the same directory with `AGENTS.md` blurred the distinction; separating them clarifies responsibility.

**Why `_llm_preamble.md` goes.** It's vestigial — a pre-v2.9.0 generator template with no live consumers. Removing it reduces surface area and matches fail-forward discipline.

**Consequences.**

- `intent/llm/AGENTS.md` deleted in WP10.
- `lib/templates/llm/_llm_preamble.md` deleted in WP10.
- Projects missing `intent/llm/DECISION_TREE.md` (9 of 15 per audit) get it planted during WP16 rollout.

## D5. working-with-llms.md is the narrative tech note

**Decision.** A new doc at `intent/docs/working-with-llms.md` carries the long-form narrative for humans: the three-file canon, hook mechanics, critic cadence, Socrates/Diogenes FAQ, how the pieces fit together.

**Why.** `usage-rules.md` is too terse for "why" content; `AGENTS.md` is auto-generated and can't carry opinion; `CLAUDE.md` is Claude-specific. A separate tech note is the natural home for system-level explanation. Matches Intent's existing pattern: `intent/docs/` holds authoring guides (`rules.md`, `critics.md`, `writing-extensions.md`), this fits the slot.

**Consequences.**

- Cross-referenced from README, AGENTS.md template, CLAUDE.md template, and usage-rules.md template.
- Owns the Socrates/Diogenes FAQ (WP12). Agent `.md` files link here for clarity.
- Owns the hook architecture narrative (WP04 ships the template; this doc explains it).

## D6. Socrates and Diogenes are two agents, two domains

**Decision.** Keep both agents. Do not rename, do not consolidate, do not split further. Add a one-paragraph FAQ sidebar to `working-with-llms.md` explaining the distinction and cross-reference it from both `agent.md` files.

**Why.** Git log forensics settle the question. Socrates debuted 2025-08-05 (commit `7f4529e`) as CTO Review Mode — personas Socrates (CTO) + Plato (Tech Lead) — for architectural and strategic decisions. Diogenes debuted 2026-02-20 (commit `37a0ed0`) as a separate new agent — personas Aristotle (Empiricist) + Diogenes (Skeptic) — for test-specification dialogs and test-quality validation. They were never the same agent. Zero overlap in domain, personas, artefacts, or triggers.

**User confusion source.** Both use Socratic dialog methodology; both have Greek-philosopher names. The Socratic-method framing conflates them. The agents solve disjoint problems.

**Why not rename.** A rename like `socrates-test` would falsely imply they're variants; they're not. Consolidating into "Socrates with modes" would force unrelated personas into one agent. Keep the clean separation and fix the documentation instead.

**Consequences.**

- FAQ paragraph in WP03 (`working-with-llms.md`) + WP12 cross-refs.
- No code change to either agent.

## D7. Session hooks inject reminders — they do not force execution

**Decision.** `.claude/settings.json` ships two primary hooks for every Intent project:

- `SessionStart` (matchers: `startup`, `resume`, `clear`, `compact`) → stdout injects a system-reminder: _"Intent project detected. Run `/in-session` before your first response to load coding standards and rules."_
- `Stop` (after every assistant turn when the user has no queued prompt) → stdout injects: _"When wrapping up, run `/in-finish` to update ST docs + wip.md + commit."_

**Why soft reminder vs hard gate.** Claude Code's hook system does not let a hook directly invoke a slash command. It can inject text into the conversation via stdout, which Claude reads as a system-reminder. In practice this has high compliance: Claude recognises critical-skill reminders and acts. A strict `UserPromptSubmit` hook that blocks the first prompt until `/in-session` has been run is possible but adds friction and can confuse users who genuinely want to ask a quick question. Soft reminder is the right default; **open decision #2** lets the user flip to strict if desired.

**Why these two events.** `SessionStart` fires reliably on new session, resume, clear, and compact. `UserPromptSubmit` fires every turn (too noisy for startup reminders). `SessionEnd` is unreliable if the user just closes the terminal. `Stop` fires after every assistant response — good for end-of-turn nudging without being annoying.

**Consequences.**

- `lib/templates/.claude/settings.json` template (WP04).
- `intent claude upgrade --apply` installs per project (WP11).
- No change to `/in-session` or `/in-finish` skills themselves — they already do the right thing; they just need to be invoked.

## D8. Critics run via git pre-commit hook as the primary gate

**Decision.** The primary critic cadence is a `.git/hooks/pre-commit` hook that runs `bin/intent_critic <lang> <staged-files>` and blocks the commit when findings exceed the configured severity threshold. Per-project override via `.intent_critic.yml` (WP07).

**Why pre-commit.** Local, deterministic, offline, zero-latency feedback. Runs on every commit on the developer's machine — catches violations before they reach a shared branch. Bypass via `git commit --no-verify` is allowed but logged so sweeps can catch bypassers.

**Secondary cadences.**

- GitHub Actions on push / PR — same `intent_critic` binary; catches bypasses and agents that push without committing locally.
- `Stop` hook's end-of-turn reminder may include "Run `/critic-<lang>` on changed files" — advisory only, not a block.

**Why not LLM-based critic for pre-commit.** The critic-\* subagents _are_ LLM-based and run via `Task(subagent_type="critic-<lang>")`. But pre-commit runs on every commit, often dozens of times a day — invoking an LLM that often is slow and costly. The rules' Detection heuristics are mechanical (YAML frontmatter + Markdown patterns), parseable in bash, deterministic. `bin/intent_critic` ports the Detection logic to bash and runs headless. The LLM subagents remain available for richer reviews via `/in-review` stage 2.

**Consequences.**

- `bin/intent_critic` is the biggest single WP in ST0035 (WP05, size L).
- `.git/hooks/pre-commit` template (WP06).
- `.intent_critic.yml` config with `severity_min: warning` default (WP07).
- `intent claude upgrade --apply` installs both into every project (WP11).

## D9. Fail-forward — no backwards-compat shims

**Decision.** Deprecated artefacts are deleted now, not kept as shadow/legacy. Migrations actively prune.

- `intent/llm/AGENTS.md` → deleted (WP10). Root `AGENTS.md` is the only location.
- `lib/templates/llm/_llm_preamble.md` → deleted (WP10). No legacy path.
- ST0010, ST0015 → `Cancelled` status via `intent st` command, moved to `intent/st/CANCELLED/`, annotated with deprecation one-liner (WP01).
- `intent agents sync` output path flips from `intent/llm/AGENTS.md` to root. Existing `intent/llm/AGENTS.md` files across the fleet are deleted during WP16 rollout.

**Why.** Intent's stated posture is fail-forward. Backwards-compat shims accumulate and decay. User instruction on this ST is explicit: "build the right thing, build the thing right, and then fail forward."

**Consequences.**

- Users running tooling that reads `intent/llm/AGENTS.md` will need to update to read root `AGENTS.md`. This is a straightforward change.
- Rollout verification (WP17) includes checks that legacy files are gone (not just that new files are present).

## D10. Phase 0 review gate before implementation

**Decision.** ST0035 starts with a Phase 0 that populates all ST and WP docs without changing any production file. Phase 0 output is committed and reviewed by the user. WP01 does not begin until Phase 0 is approved.

**Why.** Matches Intent's "document first, code next" discipline. Locks scope and acceptance criteria before any implementation decision can drift. Gives the user a single review surface (ST0035/info.md + design.md + 17 × WP/NN/info.md) rather than a stream of commits that keep moving the target.

**Consequences.**

- Phase 0 is itself sized M and commits a non-trivial amount of documentation before any code changes.
- Open decisions surfaced in this design are explicit; the review gate is where they resolve.
- Once approved, WP01 begins with the version bump + ST cancellations — the lightest-touch WP to confirm tooling works as expected.

## Risk register

| Risk                                                                                    | Severity | Likelihood | Mitigation                                                                                                                                                     |
| --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------- | ---------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `bin/intent_critic` parsing diverges from subagent Detection logic                      | High     | Med        | WP05 acceptance test: same file, same rules — subagent output and bash output produce identical findings lists                                                 |
| Hook reminder gets ignored by Claude (low compliance)                                   | Med      | Low        | Soft-reminder baseline is fail-safe (no worse than status quo). If compliance lower than expected, flip to hard gate (open decision #2 path).                  |
| Pre-commit hook blocks commits incorrectly (false positives)                            | High     | Med        | WP05 includes a known-bad / known-good test suite. `--no-verify` available as escape hatch. Users can tune `severity_min` per project.                         |
| Rollout breaks a project's existing tooling                                             | Med      | Med        | Canary in 3 projects (WP15) before sweep. `intent claude upgrade --apply` is idempotent; rollback is `git reset`.                                              |
| Deleted `intent/llm/AGENTS.md` breaks tooling that reads the old path                   | Med      | Low        | Fail-forward by instruction; verify tooling post-rollout (WP17 checklist). Document in CHANGELOG.                                                              |
| Cancelled ST directory convention doesn't match actual tooling expectations             | Low      | Low        | `intent/st/CANCELLED/` exists and is already used by `intent st` — verified during Phase 0.                                                                    |
| Elixir `mix usage_rules.sync` conflicts with Intent's refreshed usage-rules.md template | Med      | Low        | Intent's template is hand-authored, not generated by `mix usage_rules.sync`. Elixir deps' rules remain separate. Verify in WP15 canary with an Elixir project. |
| Worker-bee extension still required by users who relied on it pre-v2.9.0                | Low      | Low        | Worker-bee was relocated in v2.9.0 (not v2.9.1). Users already have the install path. No ST0035 regression.                                                    |
| Version bump choice (2.9.1 vs 2.10.0) drags the whole ST                                | Low      | Low        | Open decision #1; answer before WP01. Default 2.9.1 is safe.                                                                                                   |

## Fail-forward posture

Throughout: no deprecation stubs, no preservation of removed content, no backwards-compat env vars, no legacy paths. If a file is deleted, it is deleted. If a command's output path changes, it changes. If a migration prunes installed artefacts, it prunes. Users who need the old behaviour can pin to v2.9.0.

## Definition of done for ST0035

Applies when WP17 closes:

- All 17 projects in rollout scope have `intent_version: 2.9.1` in `.intent/config.json`.
- All 17 projects have root `AGENTS.md` (real file), root `CLAUDE.md` (templated), root `usage-rules.md` (templated), `.claude/settings.json` (hooks wired), `.git/hooks/pre-commit` (executable, critic runner), `.intent_critic.yml` (config present).
- All 17 projects have `intent/llm/MODULES.md` and `intent/llm/DECISION_TREE.md`; none have `intent/llm/AGENTS.md`.
- `intent/docs/working-with-llms.md` is live and cross-referenced from root docs.
- Socrates/Diogenes FAQ is present in `working-with-llms.md` and referenced from both `agent.md` files.
- `bin/intent_critic` exists, passes its test suite, and produces identical findings to `Task(subagent_type="critic-<lang>")` on a reference input.
- ST0010 and ST0015 are cancelled + annotated, residing in `intent/st/CANCELLED/`.
- Dogfood journal at `intent/st/ST0035/WP/17/dogfood-journal.md` records per-project outcomes.
- CHANGELOG v2.9.1 entry merged.
